reviews.text: its true. she never takes her own fault and always thinks it the students fault. "you guys have to start reading the instructions more carefully" nah we do u just not clear

Yo, don't take this class.. Karen isn't really a good teacher as she is quite new to the school, but that isn't an excuse to blame students for her own mistakes. She recently enacted a new grading policy in which she would not change grades lower than a 50. Bro, just no.

here's a very honest review:
- she's very nice but expects a lot of participation
- relatively easy class if you do all the work (just make sure you put it a LOT of detail about sensory details and random stuff like that)
- she takes off points if you don't have enough detail
- you can't really learn in this class
- gets off task really easily and takes up most of the classtime talking about something irrelevant and not related to the topic
- only take her if you absolutely have to and cannot pick any other english class please.

Extreme white savior complex. Blames you for her own inaction and mistakes. Expects you to step up to fix the school's greater issues, but refuses to do anything herself besides passively play messenger.
she two faced as hell how she gonna act nice in person but on zoom she hella rude like 😒

Pick someone else PLEASE!!! She takes her frustration out on the students which is UNBEARABLE at times... like come on now, have some class. She grades people off because of HER mistakes and she’s not clear with instructions. Email her about an assignment and expect a response the day it’s due...

she actually improved a lot since the start of when she started teaching at lowell. she is getting better at being clear about assignments (which she was lacking of the semester before) there is an ok amount of work (maybe average for a lowell class) and she is overall very nice to all her students.

unclear instructions, but class itself was pretty easy. a lot of projects but not a lot of daily homework. had her during her first semester at lowell, not your best option though. make sure to actually read romeo and juliet, she tested us and let's say not everyone did so well. also essays/paragraphs, have a good argument or she'll mark you down. kind of a tough grader on projects too. make things look neat. if it's something small that you think can be fixed, go talk to her and she'll give a few points back on your grade. she rounded my grade up to an A for one of the grading periods and got an A for the semester. good luck :)
Oh nah if u take her its commonly accepted that you had last pick.
